Event: Realizes which math teaching approaches work and which don’t
Explanation: Improves his own teaching effectiveness while helping students learn.

Event: Teaches probability using dice games
Explanation: Makes math engaging and successful for students while affirming his role as an effective teacher.

Event: Students ask to “practice probabilities”
Explanation: They request more learning that benefits them and reinforces the teacher’s positive method.

Event: Becomes a teacher because he isn’t fit to be a cop
Explanation: Choosing a role better suited to his abilities benefits his life and gives society a competent teacher.

Event: Punches his superior (Valchek) in the face
Explanation: Violently attacking his boss risks his own career while harming another without clear gain.

Event: Calls out Valchek with violence after being pushed too far
Explanation: Snapping and attacking provides no clear lasting benefit and causes professional and interpersonal damage.

Event: Improves as a policeman with guidance
Explanation: Personal skill growth makes him more competent and useful to the department.

Event: Starts teaching naive and overwhelmed, then learns to be a reasonable authority figure
Explanation: Gains confidence and skill that simultaneously help his career and his students’ classroom experience.

Event: Tries to be authoritative yet respectful as a teacher
Explanation: Balanced discipline and respect build his authority while benefiting student learning and climate.

Event: Becomes a desk jockey instead of a beat cop
Explanation: Move away from a role he’s bad at to one he’s good at improves his value to the unit and his own safety/career.

Event: Proves very good at paperwork and desk work
Explanation: Using his strengths boosts casework while supporting colleagues.

Event: Recklessly fires a gun into a wall after not clearing the chamber
Explanation: Shows dangerous incompetence with firearms, risking himself and others for no benefit.

Event: Becomes a much more confident authority figure by his final appearance
Explanation: Growing into firm authority improves his career stability and students’ structure.

Event: Acts as a “father to his men” (teacher version)
Explanation: Providing paternal care and guidance strengthens students while giving him purpose and fulfillment.

Event: Mistakes a fellow cop for a gangster and kills him by mistake
Explanation: Disastrous misjudgment ends a life and destroys his own police career with no gain.

Event: Uses data-analysis and paper-chasing skills discovered by Lester
Explanation: Applying analytical talent advances his standing while greatly helping the case.

Event: Lester mentors Prez and uncovers his analytical aptitude
Explanation: Investing in Prez’s development pays off for Lester’s team and Prez’s career.

Event: Prez improves over time, reducing his “idiot ball” behavior
Explanation: Learning from mistakes enhances his reliability and reduces burdens on others.

Event: Other cops lambaste Prez as a screw-up after the shooting
Explanation: They socially protect themselves and their narrative at Prez’s emotional expense.

Event: Fails to identify plainclothes officer and rushes in, killing him
Explanation: Panicked, planless action kills a colleague and ends his own police career.

Event: Has his cop career largely because of Valchek’s nepotism
Explanation: Valchek helps family status while saddling the force and Prez with a poor fit.

Event: Grows tired of nepotistic protection and declines to mount a defense after last accident
Explanation: Accepting responsibility and exiting a bad-fit career lets him pursue something healthier for him and safer for others.

Event: Pursues a new career as a teacher after leaving the force
Explanation: Choosing teaching unlocks his strengths and provides substantial benefit to children.

Event: Becomes a caring person as a teacher in Season 4
Explanation: Developing genuine care improves student welfare and his own fulfillment.

Event: Acts as the only seemingly competent person on the initial Sobotka detail
Explanation: His relative competence advances the case and bolsters his reputation.

Event: Takes pleased satisfaction as the Sobotka case pieces come together
Explanation: His analytical success is gratifying personally and advances the collective investigation.

Event: Cracks Barksdale pager codes and organizational structure
Explanation: Highly valuable analytical work strongly boosts both his status and the case’s progress.

Event: Improves as a cop (“level in badass”) then later backslides
Explanation: Some growth benefits him and others but later regression, including the shooting, undercuts gains more for others than him.

Event: Takes a “level in badass” as a teacher
Explanation: Becoming more capable and confident in the classroom helps his career and student outcomes.

Event: Is covered for by the police force after hurting Black people
Explanation: Protecting him avoids institutional fallout while greatly harming justice and community trust.

Event: Feels “My God, what have I done?” after killing Waggoner
Explanation: Crushing remorse damages his own well-being while slightly humanizing him to others.

Event: Avoids serious discipline for shooting his squad car and blinding a kid; is relegated to desk duty
Explanation: He lands in a role he likes and excels at while the victim and fairness suffer.

Event: Enjoys and excels at desk duty once removed from the street
Explanation: Flourishing in analysis aids investigations and stabilizes his career.

Event: Punches a superior officer and faces no real consequences
Explanation: He vents and seemingly suffers little, while undermining authority and norms.

Event: Kills another cop yet faces no criminal charges and only loses his job
Explanation: He avoids prison and reinvents as teacher while justice for the victim and trust in the system are harmed.

Event: Becomes a teacher after losing his police job and bounces back
Explanation: Transforms a career-ending failure into a socially valuable new profession.

Event: Plays Johnny Cash while working on investigations and classroom
Explanation: Music helps maintain his focus and mood, mildly benefiting shared work environments.

Event: Acknowledges he may have internalized racism that contributed to shooting a Black cop
Explanation: Self-examination can reduce future bias, helping his growth and those he serves.

Event: Pistol-whips a 14-year-old in the eye, blinding him
Explanation: Extreme, needless violence permanently harms the child and severely damages his own reputation.

Event: Later cannot look the blinded kid in the eye when they meet again
Explanation: His shame hurts him and denies the victim possible acknowledgment or apology.

Event: Eventually teaches kids the same age he once hurt
Explanation: Channeling guilt into caring teaching benefits him and similarly aged children.
